text: Good evening. Take a breath. This is not a performance review; it is a quiet look at how you showed up today.
How would you describe today in one word?
options: Productive|Challenging|Mixed|Draining

text: Route based on the employee's overall day description.
options: answer=Productive|Mixed:A1_Q_AGENCY_HIGH;answer=Challenging|Draining:A1_Q_AGENCY_LOW

text: You said "{A1_OPEN.answer}". When something went well today, what most likely made it happen?
options: I prepared well|I adapted quickly|The team supported me|The situation just worked out

text: You said "{A1_OPEN.answer}". When things got hard today, what was your first move?
options: I looked for what I could control|I pushed through alone|I waited for things to settle|I felt blocked and stuck

text: Separate active agency from circumstance-based explanations.
options: answer=I prepared well|I adapted quickly:A1_Q_CHOICE_INTERNAL;answer=The team supported me|The situation just worked out:A1_Q_CHOICE_EXTERNAL

text: Separate active response from passive or stuck response.
options: answer=I looked for what I could control|I pushed through alone:A1_Q_CHOICE_INTERNAL;answer=I waited for things to settle|I felt blocked and stuck:A1_Q_CHOICE_EXTERNAL

text: Think of one decision you made today. What guided that choice?
options: What was right for the work|What would help the team|My own judgment and experience|What someone told me to do

text: Think of one frustrating moment. Looking back, was there a small move you could have made?
options: Yes, I see one now|Maybe, with more support|Probably not, it was out of my hands|I am not sure yet

text: Route to Axis 1 reflection based on degree of agency.
options: answer=What was right for the work|What would help the team|My own judgment and experience:A1_R_INTERNAL;answer=What someone told me to do:A1_R_MIXED

text: Route to Axis 1 reflection based on whether the person can now see a choice.
options: answer=Yes, I see one now|Maybe, with more support:A1_R_MIXED;answer=Probably not, it was out of my hands|I am not sure yet:A1_R_EXTERNAL

text: You connected your actions to the outcome today. That is agency: not controlling everything, but noticing where your response mattered.
You saw some agency today, even if it was not obvious in the moment. Sometimes control is just one small next move.
Today felt like something that happened to you. That is worth noticing without blame. The useful question is: where was even one small choice?
Now that you have looked at how you handled the day, let us look at what you gave to the work and the people around you.
Now let us shift from control to contribution: what did you give today, and what were you hoping to receive?
After a difficult day, it is natural to notice what went wrong. Now let us look at what you gave, even in a small way.
Think about one interaction you had today. What was your main focus in that moment?
options: Making sure they had what they needed|Getting what I needed from them|Being heard and understood|Getting through it quickly

text: Route based on contribution orientation versus expectation orientation.
options: answer=Making sure they had what they needed|Being heard and understood:A2_Q_CONTRIBUTION;answer=Getting what I needed from them|Getting through it quickly:A2_Q_ENTITLEMENT

text: When you helped, listened, or supported someone today, what was driving that?
options: I noticed they needed help|It made the work better for everyone|It felt like the right thing to do|I wanted to be seen as helpful

text: When you needed support, recognition, or a response today and did not get it, what went through your mind?
options: I noticed the gap but moved on|I wondered what I could do differently|It felt unfair given my effort|I started thinking about who owed what

text: Separate genuine contribution from image-driven contribution.
options: answer=I noticed they needed help|It made the work better for everyone|It felt like the right thing to do:A2_Q_EXTRA_CONTRIBUTION;answer=I wanted to be seen as helpful:A2_R_MIXED

text: Separate self-regulation from entitlement ledger thinking.
options: answer=I noticed the gap but moved on|I wondered what I could do differently:A2_R_MIXED;answer=It felt unfair given my effort|I started thinking about who owed what:A2_Q_EXTRA_ENTITLEMENT

text: Was there a moment today where you did something beyond your formal responsibility?
options: Yes, and it felt natural|Yes, but I felt a little resentful|No, I stayed in my lane|No, but I wish I had stepped in

text: When you compare what you gave today with what you received, how does it feel?
options: Balanced enough|I gave more than I got|I got what I needed|I am not sure I contributed as much as I could have

text: Route to Axis 2 reflection after extra contribution question.
options: answer=Yes, and it felt natural|Yes, but I felt a little resentful:A2_R_CONTRIBUTION;answer=No, I stayed in my lane|No, but I wish I had stepped in:A2_R_MIXED

text: Route to Axis 2 reflection after entitlement-balance question.
options: answer=Balanced enough|I am not sure I contributed as much as I could have:A2_R_MIXED;answer=I gave more than I got|I got what I needed:A2_R_ENTITLEMENT

text: You leaned toward contribution today. Not every act of value gets noticed immediately, but it still strengthens the system around you.
You moved between giving and needing today. That is human. The useful reflection is whether your next move adds value or simply waits to receive it.
A quiet ledger showed up today: what you gave versus what you got back. That is not a failure, but it is a signal to notice before it becomes your default lens.
You have looked at agency and contribution. One final lens: how wide was your circle of concern today?
You have looked at what you gave and what you needed. Now let us widen the lens to who your day affected.
You noticed what you wanted from the day. Now let us ask how much of your awareness extended beyond yourself.
When you think about today's hardest moment, who came to mind first?
options: Just me|My immediate team|One person who had it harder|The customer or end user

text: Route based on radius of concern.
options: answer=Just me:A3_Q_SELF;answer=My immediate team|One person who had it harder|The customer or end user:A3_Q_OTHER

text: When you were focused on yourself today, what were you mainly trying to protect?
options: My energy|My time|My reputation|My peace of mind

text: When you thought about others today, what did they need from you most?
options: Clarity|Support|Speed|Empathy

text: Check whether self-focus stayed narrow or opened toward purpose/support.
options: answer=My energy|My time:A3_Q_SELF_CHECK;answer=My reputation|My peace of mind:A3_Q_BRIDGE_CHECK

text: Check whether other-focus was practical or deeper perspective-taking.
options: answer=Clarity|Support|Empathy:A3_Q_OTHER_CHECK;answer=Speed:A3_Q_BRIDGE_CHECK

text: Did you notice how your actions affected someone else today?
options: Yes, and it changed what I did|Yes, but I did not act on it|Not really, I was trying to get through the day|No, it did not cross my mind

text: How connected did you feel to the people your work touched today?
options: Very connected|Somewhat connected|A little distant|Not connected

text: Looking back, how much of your energy went outward versus inward?
options: Mostly outward|Balanced|Mostly inward|Hard to say

text: Final route for self-focused path.
options: answer=Yes, and it changed what I did|Yes, but I did not act on it:A3_R_MIXED;answer=Not really, I was trying to get through the day|No, it did not cross my mind:A3_R_SELF

text: Final route for other-focused path.
options: answer=Very connected|Somewhat connected:A3_R_OTHER;answer=A little distant|Not connected:A3_R_MIXED

text: Final route for mixed radius path.
options: answer=Mostly outward|Balanced:A3_R_MIXED;answer=Mostly inward|Hard to say:A3_R_SELF

text: Today your attention stayed mostly inside your own experience. That is understandable on heavy days. Tomorrow, one small outward question could change the shape of the day.
You moved between your own needs and the needs around you. That balance matters: you cannot pour from an empty cup, but you also noticed the cups around you.
You looked beyond yourself today: toward teammates, people who struggled, or the people served by the work. That wider lens often makes difficult work more meaningful.

Today you leaned {axis1.dominant} on agency, {axis2.dominant} on contribution, and {axis3.dominant} in your radius of concern. This is not a score. It is a mirror: one clear way to notice how you showed up and one small place to grow tomorrow.
See you tomorrow. You showed up today, and that counts.
